---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 8a32a95c24be180511c0c46d8ef66f9b7b83fcf3
findings:
  - { id: judged-promotion-os-rename-vs-rename-exclusive, kind: judged, text: "ac-3 (and ac-1) frozen text says promotion is 'exactly one os.Rename of the staged store onto the real .verdi path'. The implementation promotes via renameExclusive (renamex_np/RENAME_EXCL on darwin, renameat2/RENAME_NOREPLACE on linux, refusing on unsupported platforms) — NOT os.Rename. This is intentional and disclosed in the spec's own Note (to close the empty-dir silent-replace race), and it strengthens ac-1's backstop, so intent is reconciled — but the frozen ac-1/ac-3 wording ('os.Rename') is now stale relative to the built verb and should be queued for ratification the same way spec/cli-creation's frozen ac-1 wording was. A reader trusting only the frozen ac text would mis-describe the promotion primitive. (confidence 0.50)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "The judge itself reconciles intent: promotion uses renameExclusive (RENAME_EXCL/RENAME_NOREPLACE) which is STRONGER than os.Rename (closes the empty-dir silent-replace race, refuses any existing .verdi atomically), disclosed in the spec Note. The frozen ac-1/ac-3 'os.Rename'/ENOTEMPTY wording is stale relative to the built verb and QUEUED FOR RATIFICATION (08-revision-notes at feature close) — the judge explicitly recommends this, same as spec/cli-creation's frozen ac-1. Behavior correct-and-stronger; honesty slice." }
  - { id: judged-live-validation-preview-coverage, kind: judged, text: "ac-2 says the wizard's two configurable axes are vocabulary renames AND a class's template-file choice, and that 'every answer is previewed live by validating the in-progress candidate model in memory ... before the interview moves on.' Only the vocabulary rename answers receive an in-interview live validation/preview (runRenamePrompt renders+DecodeModel and prints '-\u003e valid'); the template-copy yes/no answer (the second frontier axis) and the structural-request answer get no live candidate-model preview at answer time — the copied template overrides are only validated later, at the promotion gate (runModelCheck's checkTemplates), not 'before the interview moves on.' Defensible reading: the template copy is byte-identical and does not alter the in-memory model, so a per-answer model preview would be a no-op; but under a strict reading of 'every answer is previewed live', the second axis's answer is not previewed in-interview. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Defensible-reading nuance the judge names itself: only vocabulary-rename answers alter the in-memory candidate model, so they get the live DecodeModel preview; the template-copy answer is byte-identical (no model change → a per-answer model preview would be a no-op) and is validated at the promotion gate via runModelCheck's checkTemplates; the structural-request answer is refused by the frontier, not a model value. The no-unvalidated-mutation intent holds (only validated answers apply). Same class as the build-round judged-preview-every-answer accepted-deviation. Honesty slice, not a fix-class defect." }
not-resurfaced:
  - { id: judged-frontier-refusal-request-channel, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's frontier refusal ('a request for capability outside that frontier … is refused … and the interview continues') is implemented as a fixed, always-asked yes/no probe question that every interview poses ('Add, remove, or restructure the class hierarchy…?', internal/initwizard/interview.go:104) rather than as a response to an operator-initiated request. This satisfies the refuse-and-continue behavior when answered 'yes', but it means the interview surface is not 'exactly the v1 frontier's two configurable axes' — a third, out-of-frontier solicitation is part of every run and every stdin script must answer it — and a structural request expressed anywhere else (e.g. typed as free text at a rename prompt) is silently accepted as a display word, never refused with the frontier explanation. (confidence 0.40)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "A deterministic CLI cannot detect structural intent in free text; the explicit probe IS the honest v1 request channel; rename prompts accept typed text as display words by their own contract." }
  - { id: judged-declined-write-exit-code, kind: judged, text: "The wizard still poses a final 'Write .verdi/ ?' confirmation (internal/initwizard/interview.go:117-123) that no AC describes — ac-2 scopes the interview to the two configurable axes, and ac-3's abort taxonomy covers only stdin-ends-early and staging crashes. The earlier exit-code half of this finding is resolved: a deliberate decline (ErrDeclinedWrite) now exits 0 with a plain stdout line and the staged temp discarded (cmd/verdi/init.go:142-145), a defensible 'clean no-op' classification under the module's 0/1/2 discipline. What remains is the unauthorized prompt itself plus a whole decline outcome (exit 0, 'nothing was written') that exists outside every AC, and which any stdin script written from the spec alone would fail to answer, hitting ErrAborted instead. (confidence 0.25)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Write confirmation kept-and-disclosed (spec-body Note); decline is a clean exit-0 no-op — judge-acknowledged defensible under the 0/1/2 discipline; the prompt is a disclosed interview-flow addition beyond the AC letter." }
  - { id: judged-crash-injection-fidelity, kind: judged, text: "ac-3's 'simulated crash injected partway through staging' is implemented as an ordinary error return (simulateCrashAfter, cmd/verdi/init.go:338-343) that still executes the deferred os.RemoveAll(tempRoot) — so the built-binary evidence proves the error-path cleanup, not survival of an actual process death, under which the deferred discard never runs and a populated .verdi-init-tmp-* wrapper persists beside the target. The load-bearing invariant (no .verdi/ ever appears at the real root) does still hold by construction since no real-root write precedes the single rename, and the AC's own wording attributes the guarantee to discard-on-error, so this is a fidelity gap in what the crash pin demonstrates rather than a violated outcome. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "The pin proves discard-on-error — exactly what the AC letter attributes; true process death leaves at most an inert sibling temp dir, never a partial .verdi, by construction (no real-root write precedes the single rename)." }
digest: sha256:daa4f4df483b7c2de2df2abbc8f24b56c33e6bfef84daa994d3d003365b9a705
integrity: sha256:ef764abc30644f617dee0ba46ede3570debaa9c916149dc4e8f8e918ce4e5f4c
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "Based on my review of the spec's acceptance criteria against the implementation (`cmd/verdi/init.go`, `internal/initwizard/*`, and the `internal/model` frontier/decode path it delegates to), the implementation is largely faithful: the existence-refusal is unified on any `.verdi/` entry, staging happens in a same-filesystem sibling temp dir, the promotion gate runs the full `runModelCheck` core (not decode-only), `RenameableIDs` computes exactly the same legal-key set as `validateVocabulary`, and the live rename preview runs `DecodeModel` (kernel `Validate` + `checkFrontier`), matching ac-2's stated scope. The genuine intent divergences a graph diff won't surface:\n\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"promotion-os-rename-vs-rename-exclusive\",\"text\":\"ac-3 (and ac-1) frozen text says promotion is 'exactly one os.Rename of the staged store onto the real .verdi path'. The implementation promotes via renameExclusive (renamex_np/RENAME_EXCL on darwin, renameat2/RENAME_NOREPLACE on linux, refusing on unsupported platforms) — NOT os.Rename. This is intentional and disclosed in the spec's own Note (to close the empty-dir silent-replace race), and it strengthens ac-1's backstop, so intent is reconciled — but the frozen ac-1/ac-3 wording ('os.Rename') is now stale relative to the built verb and should be queued for ratification the same way spec/cli-creation's frozen ac-1 wording was. A reader trusting only the frozen ac text would mis-describe the promotion primitive.\",\"confidence\":0.5},{\"id\":\"live-validation-preview-coverage\",\"text\":\"ac-2 says the wizard's two configurable axes are vocabulary renames AND a class's template-file choice, and that 'every answer is previewed live by validating the in-progress candidate model in memory ... before the interview moves on.' Only the vocabulary rename answers receive an in-interview live validation/preview (runRenamePrompt renders+DecodeModel and prints '-\u003e valid'); the template-copy yes/no answer (the second frontier axis) and the structural-request answer get no live candidate-model preview at answer time — the copied template overrides are only validated later, at the promotion gate (runModelCheck's checkTemplates), not 'before the interview moves on.' Defensible reading: the template copy is byte-identical and does not alter the in-memory model, so a per-answer model preview would be a no-op; but under a strict reading of 'every answer is previewed live', the second axis's answer is not previewed in-interview.\",\"confidence\":0.3}]}" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-22, commit: 8a32a95c24be180511c0c46d8ef66f9b7b83fcf3 }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/init-wizard@8a32a95c24be180511c0c46d8ef66f9b7b83fcf3, spec/init-wizard@a28bc2178eb7f481bc30b2b234a3d5e944b9591a], digest: sha256:daa4f4df483b7c2de2df2abbc8f24b56c33e6bfef84daa994d3d003365b9a705, integrity: sha256:ef764abc30644f617dee0ba46ede3570debaa9c916149dc4e8f8e918ce4e5f4c, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-promotion-os-rename-vs-rename-exclusive** [accepted-deviation]: ac-3 (and ac-1) frozen text says promotion is 'exactly one os.Rename of the staged store onto the real .verdi path'. The implementation promotes via renameExclusive (renamex_np/RENAME_EXCL on darwin, renameat2/RENAME_NOREPLACE on linux, refusing on unsupported platforms) — NOT os.Rename. This is intentional and disclosed in the spec's own Note (to close the empty-dir silent-replace race), and it strengthens ac-1's backstop, so intent is reconciled — but the frozen ac-1/ac-3 wording ('os.Rename') is now stale relative to the built verb and should be queued for ratification the same way spec/cli-creation's frozen ac-1 wording was. A reader trusting only the frozen ac text would mis-describe the promotion primitive. (confidence 0.50) — The judge itself reconciles intent: promotion uses renameExclusive (RENAME_EXCL/RENAME_NOREPLACE) which is STRONGER than os.Rename (closes the empty-dir silent-replace race, refuses any existing .verdi atomically), disclosed in the spec Note. The frozen ac-1/ac-3 'os.Rename'/ENOTEMPTY wording is stale relative to the built verb and QUEUED FOR RATIFICATION (08-revision-notes at feature close) — the judge explicitly recommends this, same as spec/cli-creation's frozen ac-1. Behavior correct-and-stronger; honesty slice.
- **judged-live-validation-preview-coverage** [accepted-deviation]: ac-2 says the wizard's two configurable axes are vocabulary renames AND a class's template-file choice, and that 'every answer is previewed live by validating the in-progress candidate model in memory ... before the interview moves on.' Only the vocabulary rename answers receive an in-interview live validation/preview (runRenamePrompt renders+DecodeModel and prints '-> valid'); the template-copy yes/no answer (the second frontier axis) and the structural-request answer get no live candidate-model preview at answer time — the copied template overrides are only validated later, at the promotion gate (runModelCheck's checkTemplates), not 'before the interview moves on.' Defensible reading: the template copy is byte-identical and does not alter the in-memory model, so a per-answer model preview would be a no-op; but under a strict reading of 'every answer is previewed live', the second axis's answer is not previewed in-interview. (confidence 0.30) — Defensible-reading nuance the judge names itself: only vocabulary-rename answers alter the in-memory candidate model, so they get the live DecodeModel preview; the template-copy answer is byte-identical (no model change → a per-answer model preview would be a no-op) and is validated at the promotion gate via runModelCheck's checkTemplates; the structural-request answer is refused by the frontier, not a model value. The no-unvalidated-mutation intent holds (only validated answers apply). Same class as the build-round judged-preview-every-answer accepted-deviation. Honesty slice, not a fix-class defect.

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)

## Not resurfaced

- **judged-frontier-refusal-request-channel** [not-resurfaced, was accepted-deviation]: ac-2's frontier refusal ('a request for capability outside that frontier … is refused … and the interview continues') is implemented as a fixed, always-asked yes/no probe question that every interview poses ('Add, remove, or restructure the class hierarchy…?', internal/initwizard/interview.go:104) rather than as a response to an operator-initiated request. This satisfies the refuse-and-continue behavior when answered 'yes', but it means the interview surface is not 'exactly the v1 frontier's two configurable axes' — a third, out-of-frontier solicitation is part of every run and every stdin script must answer it — and a structural request expressed anywhere else (e.g. typed as free text at a rename prompt) is silently accepted as a display word, never refused with the frontier explanation. (confidence 0.40) — A deterministic CLI cannot detect structural intent in free text; the explicit probe IS the honest v1 request channel; rename prompts accept typed text as display words by their own contract.
- **judged-declined-write-exit-code** [not-resurfaced, was accepted-deviation]: The wizard still poses a final 'Write .verdi/ ?' confirmation (internal/initwizard/interview.go:117-123) that no AC describes — ac-2 scopes the interview to the two configurable axes, and ac-3's abort taxonomy covers only stdin-ends-early and staging crashes. The earlier exit-code half of this finding is resolved: a deliberate decline (ErrDeclinedWrite) now exits 0 with a plain stdout line and the staged temp discarded (cmd/verdi/init.go:142-145), a defensible 'clean no-op' classification under the module's 0/1/2 discipline. What remains is the unauthorized prompt itself plus a whole decline outcome (exit 0, 'nothing was written') that exists outside every AC, and which any stdin script written from the spec alone would fail to answer, hitting ErrAborted instead. (confidence 0.25) — Write confirmation kept-and-disclosed (spec-body Note); decline is a clean exit-0 no-op — judge-acknowledged defensible under the 0/1/2 discipline; the prompt is a disclosed interview-flow addition beyond the AC letter.
- **judged-crash-injection-fidelity** [not-resurfaced, was accepted-deviation]: ac-3's 'simulated crash injected partway through staging' is implemented as an ordinary error return (simulateCrashAfter, cmd/verdi/init.go:338-343) that still executes the deferred os.RemoveAll(tempRoot) — so the built-binary evidence proves the error-path cleanup, not survival of an actual process death, under which the deferred discard never runs and a populated .verdi-init-tmp-* wrapper persists beside the target. The load-bearing invariant (no .verdi/ ever appears at the real root) does still hold by construction since no real-root write precedes the single rename, and the AC's own wording attributes the guarantee to discard-on-error, so this is a fidelity gap in what the crash pin demonstrates rather than a violated outcome. (confidence 0.30) — The pin proves discard-on-error — exactly what the AC letter attributes; true process death leaves at most an inert sibling temp dir, never a partial .verdi, by construction (no real-root write precedes the single rename).
